text: You have power over your mind, not outside events. Realize this, and you will find strength.
author: Marcus Aurelius

text: Discipline is choosing between what you want now and what you want most.
author: Abraham Lincoln

text: The only way to do great work is to love what you do.
author: Steve Jobs

text: Focus on being productive instead of busy.
author: Tim Ferriss

text: It is not that we have a short time to live, but that we waste a lot of it.
author: Seneca

text: The secret of getting ahead is getting started.
author: Mark Twain

text: Your time is limited, so don't waste it living someone else's life.
author: Steve Jobs

text: The future depends on what you do today.
author: Mahatma Gandhi

text: Amor Fati — love your fate and what happens to you.
author: Epictetus

text: Success is the sum of small efforts, repeated day in and day out.
author: Robert Collier

text: Do not wait to strike till the iron is hot, but make it hot by striking.
author: William Butler Yeats

text: The world is full of people waiting for someone to tell them what to do.
author: John Milton

text: If you spend too much time thinking about a thing, you'll never get it done.
author: Bruce Lee

text: First say to yourself what you would be, and then do what you have to do.
author: Epictetus

text: He who has a why to live can bear almost any how.
author: Friedrich Nietzsche

text: You must be the change you wish to see in the world.
author: Mahatma Gandhi

text: The best time to plant a tree was 20 years ago. The second best time is now.
author: Chinese Proverb

text: We suffer more often in imagination than in reality.
author: Seneca

text: Starve your distractions. Feed your focus.
author: Unknown

text: Consistency is what transforms average into excellence.
author: Unknown

text: The obstacle is the way.
author: Marcus Aurelius

text: Do something today that your future self will thank you for.
author: Sean Patrick Flanery

text: Don't watch the clock; do what it does. Keep going.
author: Sam Levenson

text: It does not matter how slowly you go as long as you do not stop.
author: Confucius

text: No great thing is created suddenly, any more than a bunch of grapes or a fig.
author: Epictetus

text: The only limit to our realization of tomorrow is our doubts of today.
author: Franklin D. Roosevelt

text: Knowing is not enough; we must apply. Willing is not enough; we must do.
author: Johann Wolfgang von Goethe

text: He who would accomplish little must sacrifice little; he who would accomplish much must sacrifice much.
author: James Allen

text: The more I learn, the more I realize how little I know.
author: Socrates

text: A year from now you may wish you had started today.
author: Karen Lamb

text: He who is not courageous enough to take risks will accomplish nothing in life.
author: Muhammad Ali

text: The happiest people don't necessarily have the best of everything; they make the best of everything.
author: Unknown

text: You don't have to be great to start, but you have to start to be great.
author: Zig Ziglar

text: Action is the foundational key to all success.
author: Pablo Picasso

text: Be tolerant with others and strict with yourself.
author: Marcus Aurelius

text: The secret of life is knowing when to stop.
author: Leonard Bernstein

text: Nothing is particularly hard if you divide it into small jobs.
author: Henry Ford

text: The best revenge is massive success.
author: Frank Sinatra

text: Make each day your masterpiece.
author: John Wooden

text: Fate leads the willing, and drags the unwilling.
author: Seneca

text: Efficiency is doing things right. Effectiveness is doing the right things.
author: Peter Drucker

text: What we fear of doing most is usually what we most need to do.
author: Tim Ferriss

text: Wealth is not in having more possessions, but in having fewer wants.
author: Epictetus

text: Only put off until tomorrow what you are willing to die having left undone.
author: Pablo Picasso

text: The person who says it cannot be done should not interrupt the person doing it.
author: Chinese Proverb

text: Life is really simple, but we insist on making it complicated.
author: Confucius

text: A goal without a plan is only a wish.
author: Antoine de Saint-Exupéry

text: The strongest characters are forged in the hottest fires.
author: Unknown

text: The most difficult thing is the decision to act. The rest is merely tenacity.
author: Amelia Earhart

text: Simplicity is the ultimate sophistication.
author: Leonardo da Vinci

text: If you can't stop thinking about it, stop wasting time and start working on it.
author: Unknown
